macro_segments.title: Father's Retention of Produce Rights When Transferring Land to Son

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Father retains rights to harvested fruit; son inherits unharvested fruit on tree
Gemara reconciles Mishnah with Baraita: distinction between son and third-party purchaser
Father's intentions toward son affect fruit rights; closeness of relationship (da'ato)

macro_segments.title: Estate Division Among Adult and Minor Sons: Clothing and Food Expenses

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Older sons cannot burden estate for clothing; younger sons cannot for food
Exception: Estate may pay for oldest son's clothing if he represents family interests
Marriage expenses: if father paid for older sons' weddings before death, younger sons cannot claim equal amounts from estate

macro_segments.title: Estate Division Among Adult and Minor Daughters: Identical Rules to Sons with Added Distinction

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Daughters face same clothing/food allocation rules as sons
Key difference: daughters receive support from estate when sons inherit

macro_segments.title: Husband's Status Regarding Wife's Property (Nichsei Milog): Purchaser or Heir?

micro_segments.title: Question posed: Can creditors of wife enforce liens on her property in husband's hands?
Raba applies Mishnah of daughter marriages to show husband treated as heir (yoresh)
Gemara debates whether case of daughter marriages proves point; alternative reading proposed
Ravin's tradition: widow continues support even after daughter marries and daughter dies
Evidence from Yovel laws and inheritance terminology supports husband as yoresh
Raba complicates picture: Usha enactment allows husband to reclaim property wife sold
Rav Ashi resolves: husband treated as yoresh or lokeach depending on whose benefit; different cases have different rules

macro_segments.title: Support of Daughters from Estate When Sons Inherit: Amount Threshold Determines Control

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: When estate is large (merubim), sons inherit and daughters receive support (mezuzim)
When estate is small (muatim), daughters receive support; sons go begging
Definition of 'large': enough property to support both boys and girls until girls reach maturity
Gemara: before threshold (enough for all support), money is cordoned for support, not true inheritance
Once threshold crossed, sons inherit estate; daughters have lien for support until maturity

topical_tags.term: da'ato shel adam kerova (father's intent toward son)
ba'al lokeach vs. yoresh (husband as purchaser or heir)
takana of Usha (enactment allowing husband to reclaim sold property)
nechasim merubim vs. muatim (large vs. small estates)
Rabbi Yossi bar Chanina; Rabbi Yossi berabi Chanina
